mid_skills.name: Retrieve an item from an open appliance or container
mid_skills.description: Use this when the target item is visible inside a nearby receptacle or appliance and you need to take it out to continue the task. Apply it after navigating to the receptacle and confirming it is open.
procedure: Go to the receptacle or appliance that contains the target item.
Open it if it is closed.
Take the target item from inside.
constraints: Only try to take the item after you can see it inside the open receptacle.
If the receptacle is closed, open it before attempting to pick up the item.
After heating or other processing, reopen the appliance before trying to remove the item.

mid_skills.name: Fetch, chill, and carry an item from storage to its destination
mid_skills.description: Use this when a target item is not already in hand, needs to be cooled with the fridge, and then must be delivered to another location or receptacle.
procedure: Go to the item's current location.
Take the target item.
Go to the fridge and open it if needed.
Cool the item with the fridge.
Go to the destination location.
Open the destination receptacle if needed.
Place the item at the destination.
constraints: Go to the item's current location before trying to take it.
Open the fridge before cooling with it, and open the destination receptacle before placing the item inside if it is closed.
Hold the item before cooling or placing it.
If repeated searching at nearby surfaces is not finding the item, switch to a different likely container or surface instead of revisiting the same spots.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and place a target item using the needed appliance or container
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the target item is inside a visible container or at another fixture and the task requires moving it through an intermediate appliance or into a final receptacle. Apply it after navigating to each needed location and verifying access to closed containers.
procedure: Go to the location that currently contains the target item.
Open the container there if it is closed.
Pick up the target item.
Go to the required appliance or destination receptacle.
Open it if needed before placement.
Put the target item into the required appliance or final receptacle.
If the item must continue to another destination after appliance use, take it back out and carry it to the next target location before placing it there.
constraints: Open a closed container before taking from it or placing into it.
Hold the target item before heating it or placing it into another receptacle.
If the item was heated in an appliance, reopen it and take the item back out before going to the next destination.
Do not repeat heating or wander to unrelated fixtures without a checked next placement target.

mid_skills.name: Check a container, move to the target surface, and clean up open storage
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to inspect a storage appliance or container, optionally take an item from it or elsewhere, and then relocate toward the next work area. It also applies when you have finished checking a container and should leave it closed before moving on.
procedure: Go to the relevant container or storage location.
If the container is closed, open it so its contents are visible.
Examine the container to confirm what is inside before acting.
Take the needed item if it is present and accessible.
Close the container when you are done with it and before leaving.
Go to the next location where the item will be processed or placed.
constraints: Only open or close a receptacle when you are at that receptacle.
Examine contents after opening when availability is uncertain; do not assume the needed item is inside.
If the target item is on an exposed surface instead of inside the container, pick it up there and skip unnecessary container interaction.
Before leaving an appliance or storage container, verify whether it is still open and close it if no further immediate use is needed.

mid_skills.name: Heat a held item, then carry it to the disposal target
mid_skills.description: Use this when you already have the target item in hand and need to heat it with a microwave before placing it at a destination receptacle. Apply it after pickup and before the final placement.
procedure: Check that you are holding the item.
Go to the microwave.
Heat the held item with the microwave.
Go to the destination receptacle.
Place the heated item into or onto the destination receptacle.
constraints: Hold the item before going to the microwave; check inventory if unsure.
Go directly from the microwave to the destination receptacle after heating instead of wandering between unrelated locations.
Only place the item once you have reached the intended destination receptacle.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and heat a target item for placement
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the item to place may be inside a container or resting on a visible surface, and you need to warm it before delivering it to its destination. Apply it after navigating to likely storage or surface locations and verifying where the target item is.
procedure: Go to a likely container or surface that may hold the target item.
If the location is a closed container, open it.
Examine the current container or surface to confirm whether the target item is there.
Pick up the target item from the confirmed location.
Go to the heating appliance.
Heat the held item with the appliance.
Go to the intended destination surface or receptacle.
Put the heated item there.
constraints: Open a closed container before trying to inspect or take items from it.
Verify the target item is present at the current container or surface before picking it up.
Heat the item only after you are holding it and have gone to the heating appliance.
If the current container does not contain the target item, continue checking other visible locations instead of taking unrelated objects.

mid_skills.name: Inspect placed items at a destination surface
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you have moved an item to a table or similar surface and need to verify what is on the surface or check the state of the placed item before taking further action.
procedure: Go to the destination surface where the item was placed or is expected to be.
Examine the surface to see which objects are present there.
Examine the relevant object to confirm its visible state or identity.
If the needed object is not on the surface, go to the receptacle that should contain it and open that receptacle before checking again.
Pick up the object only after you have confirmed it is the one you need.
constraints: Be at the destination surface before inspecting it or the item on it.
If the item is inside a container or appliance instead of on the surface, navigate to that receptacle and open it before trying to inspect or take the item.
If inspection shows you picked up or placed the wrong object, correct that before continuing instead of repeating the same inspect-pick-put loop.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to likely containers and open them
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to search storage or appliance locations for an item and the target may be inside a closed receptacle. It applies while moving through likely locations such as fridges, cabinets, or microwaves to reveal their contents.
procedure: Go to a likely receptacle or storage location that could contain the needed item.
Open the receptacle if it is closed.
If the target is not visible there, go to the next likely receptacle and open it.
Repeat this move-then-open pattern until the needed contents are revealed.
constraints: Only open receptacles that are present at your current location; navigate there first.
If a receptacle is already open, inspect its visible contents instead of reopening it.
Do not treat unrelated objects found during the search as the target; continue to the next likely location when the needed item is not visible.
Use this as a search phase before pickup or placement, not as a substitute for verifying the target item is present.

mid_skills.name: Approach and inspect a lit surface item
mid_skills.description: Use this when an item of interest is expected on a surface with a switchable light, or when you need to place something there before interacting with the lit area. First get to the surface, turn on the light if present, and inspect the surface before taking or handling the target item.
procedure: Go to the relevant surface or fixture area.
If you need another object on that surface first, place it there.
Toggle the nearby lamp or light on.
Look at or examine the surface to verify the visible items.
Pick up the target item from the surface once it is visible.
constraints: Only use this behavior when the target item or the object you need to place is at the same location as the surface area you navigate to.
Before picking up an item, verify it is visible on the examined surface; if it is not visible, do not assume it is there.
Only place an object on the surface if you are already holding it.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to likely item locations and collect a visible target
mid_skills.description: Use this when you need to find and pick up a target object whose exact location is not yet known, especially among visible surfaces or open receptacles. Apply it after an initial room scan or while checking candidate locations one by one.
procedure: Scan the room to identify candidate locations that could hold the target object.
Go to each plausible location in turn until you reach one where the target object is visible or the container may hold it.
If the target may be inside a closed receptacle at that location, open it before checking its contents.
When the target object is visible and reachable, pick it up from its current surface or receptacle.
If the target is not present there, continue to the next candidate location instead of repeating checks at the same empty spot.
constraints: Only pick up the object after verifying it is visible at the current location.
Open a receptacle only after navigating to it, and only when it is closed.
If opening a location does nothing or reveals nothing relevant, move on to another candidate location.
This behavior covers locating and acquiring the object; placing it elsewhere requires a separate follow-up step.

mid_skills.name: Transport held items to a target surface or container
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you have identified the destination and need to bring one or more carried items there for placement. Apply it after confirming what you are carrying and before placing each item at the target.
procedure: Go to the target receptacle or surface where the item should end up.
Inspect the target if needed to confirm it is the correct destination and usable state.
Place one carried item onto or into the target.
If another required item is not currently held, go to its known location and pick it up.
Return to the same target and place the next item.
Repeat the retrieve-and-return cycle until all required items have been placed at the target.
constraints: Verify that the item is in your inventory before trying to place it; if it is not, return to the last known source and pick it up first.
If the destination is a container, make sure it is open and visibly accessible before placing an item inside.
After placing one item, check whether another required item is still elsewhere or was left on an intermediate surface, and retrieve it before continuing.
Do not keep opening or visiting many candidate receptacles unless they are confirmed sources or the intended destination.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to and retrieve a visible target item
mid_skills.description: Use this when the target item is already visible on a nearby surface or when checking a likely container or surface for the item. Apply it to move to the candidate location, reveal contents if needed, and take the item once it is visibly present.
procedure: Go to the surface or container that is likely to hold the target item.
If the target is inside a closed container, open the container.
Take the target item from the surface or opened container.
If multiple copies are required, take each visible required copy before leaving the location.
constraints: Only try to take an item after you can see it at the current location or in the opened container.
Open a receptacle only after navigating to it, and only if it is closed.
If repeated navigation through many similar locations is not finding the target, verify the current location's contents before continuing to another one.

mid_skills.name: Search openable containers for a target item
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the needed item may be inside closed drawers, cabinets, or similar containers, and you need to locate it before moving it elsewhere.
procedure: Go to one likely container at a time.
Open the container if it is closed.
Examine the opened container to check whether the target item is inside.
If the target item is present, pick it up from the container.
If the item is not there, continue to the next likely container and repeat.
constraints: Only try this on openable containers you can navigate to.
Open a closed container before attempting to inspect or take anything from it.
After opening, verify the target item is visible before picking it up.
Stop the search phase once you have the target item; placing it elsewhere is a separate step.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve an item from a likely cold-storage source
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the needed item may be stored in a fridge or another closed receptacle and you need to obtain it before the next task step. Apply it after navigating to the storage location and verifying the target item is there.
procedure: Go to the likely storage location for the target item.
Open the receptacle if it is closed.
Check the visible contents to confirm the target item is present.
Pick up the target item from the receptacle or current surface.
constraints: Go to the storage receptacle before trying to open or take from it.
If the receptacle is closed, open it before attempting to pick up the item.
Only pick up an item after verifying it is visible in the open receptacle or at the current surface.
If the target item is not present, continue searching other likely locations instead of taking a different object.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve, heat, and place a food item
mid_skills.description: Use this when the target item is visible inside a storage receptacle and the task requires heating it before placing it on a destination surface or receptacle.
procedure: Go to the receptacle that contains the target item.
Open the receptacle if it is closed.
Pick up the target item from inside.
Go to the heating appliance.
Heat the held item with the appliance.
Go to the required destination.
Put the heated item on or in the destination.
constraints: Verify the target item is actually in the current receptacle before taking it; if not, search a different plausible location instead of opening unrelated containers repeatedly.
Open a closed receptacle before trying to take the item from it.
Hold the item before heating or placing it.
Only perform the heating step when the task requires a heated item.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve, rinse, and store an item in a cabinet
mid_skills.description: Use this when an item needs to be cleaned at the sink and then placed into a cabinet. Apply it after locating the item and when the destination cabinet is available.
procedure: Look around and check your inventory to orient yourself before searching.
Go to the item's current location and pick up the target item.
Examine the item if you need to confirm you have the right object.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the held item there.
Go to the target cabinet.
Open the cabinet if it is closed.
Put the cleaned item into the cabinet.
constraints: Hold the target item before trying to clean or store it.
Navigate to the sinkbasin before using the clean action.
If the cabinet is closed, open it before putting the item inside.
Do not treat exploratory navigation alone as completion; finish by placing the cleaned item into the cabinet.

mid_skills.name: Cool an item using the fridge
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when an item needs to be chilled with the fridge and you may need to fetch it from another surface or appliance first. Apply it once the target item is identified and the fridge location is known.
procedure: Go to the fridge.
Open the fridge if it is closed.
Go to the target item's current location.
Pick up the target item.
Return to the fridge.
Cool the item using the fridge.
Go to the intended destination surface or receptacle.
Put the item there.
constraints: Go to the fridge before trying to cool the item, and open it first if it is closed.
Hold the target item before using the fridge to cool it.
If the item must end up back on a specific support, return to that location and place it there after cooling.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve, heat, and place a target item
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a target item must be found in a container or appliance, heated with a microwave, and then transferred to a destination receptacle or surface.
procedure: Go to the container or appliance that may hold the target item.
Open it if needed and take the target item.
Go to the microwave.
Place the target item in the microwave if it is not already there.
Heat the target item with the microwave.
Open the microwave if needed and take the heated item.
Close the microwave if it is left open.
Go to the destination receptacle or surface.
Place the heated item at the destination.
constraints: Go to the relevant container or appliance before manipulating it.
Open a closed container or appliance before taking from it or placing into it.
Hold the target item before heating or placing it.
If the item is inside the microwave and the door is closed, open it before retrieving the item after heating.
Verify the destination is the intended receptacle or surface before placing the heated item.

mid_skills.name: Collect and return an item to a lit viewing spot
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a target item must be brought to a specific surface or area that provides illumination for inspection, and the item and light source may be in different locations.
procedure: Look around to identify the illuminated destination surface and the target item's current location.
Go to the destination surface that has the light source.
Turn on the light source if it is present and not already on.
Go to the location holding the target item.
Pick up the target item from its surface or container.
Return to the illuminated destination surface.
constraints: Verify the destination surface actually has the required light source before using it as the viewing spot.
If the light source is off, turn it on before bringing the item back for inspection.
Only pick up the target item after navigating to its current location and confirming it is visible there.

mid_skills.name: Go to the lit surface and switch on the desk lamp
mid_skills.description: Use this when the task involves viewing or inspecting something under a desk lamp and you have identified the surface that holds the lamp.
procedure: Go to the surface that has the desk lamp.
Look at the local surface description to confirm the desk lamp is present.
Use the desk lamp to turn it on.
constraints: Only do this after locating the specific surface that contains the desk lamp.
If the lamp is on a different surface than the target item, plan to return to the target item after switching the lamp on.
Do not treat turning on the lamp as completion; verify the object still needs to be examined under the light afterward.

mid_skills.name: Search likely item locations
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to locate a target item in a room and its exact container or surface is not yet known. It applies while moving among visible storage spots and surfaces to inspect them for the item.
procedure: Look around the room and check your inventory before starting the search.
Go to a likely container or surface for the target item.
If the location is closed, open it.
Inspect the visible contents, then move on to the next likely location until you find the target item.
constraints: Start from the room that contains the candidate containers or surfaces you plan to inspect.
Open a closed container before relying on its contents; items inside are only visible after it is open.
Do not waste steps repeatedly closing searched locations during the search unless another task requires them closed.
Verify that the target item is actually visible before switching from searching to picking it up.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve, rinse, and place a target item
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a target item must be cleaned at a sink and then set on a visible destination surface or receptacle. Apply it after locating the item and when you can navigate between its source, the sink, and the destination.
procedure: Go to the container or surface that holds the target item.
Open the container if it is closed.
Pick up the target item.
Go to the sinkbasin.
Clean the held item with the sinkbasin.
Go to the destination surface or receptacle.
Place the cleaned item at the destination.
constraints: If the item is inside a closed drawer or cabinet, open it before taking the item.
Hold the target item before trying to clean or place it.
Go directly to the known source or target location instead of opening and checking many containers in sequence.
After placing the cleaned item, verify it is now at the destination before moving on.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to likely storage and open it
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to search cabinets or similar closed storage for an item, or when you have reached a closed receptacle that may need to be accessed next. It applies during systematic movement between nearby storage locations and opening each one for inspection or use.
procedure: Go to the next likely storage receptacle.
Open the receptacle if it is closed.
Move on to another nearby storage receptacle and repeat as needed until you reach the one you need.
constraints: Go to the receptacle before trying to open it.
If the receptacle is already open, inspect or use it instead of reopening it.
Use this as a local search/access routine for closed storage; stop once the needed item or target receptacle has been found.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and warm a container for placement
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a target container must be heated before being placed at another location, and the container may be stored inside a closed receptacle. Apply it once you know the destination and have access to a heating appliance.
procedure: Go to the receptacle that contains the container.
Open the receptacle if it is closed.
Pick up the container.
Go to the heating appliance.
Heat the container with the appliance.
Go to the intended destination.
Put the heated container at the destination.
constraints: If the container is inside a closed receptacle, open that receptacle before taking it.
Hold the container before heating it; do not leave it inside the appliance as a substitute for the heat action.
After heating, navigate directly to the intended destination and place the container there instead of detouring to unrelated surfaces or rechecking it.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and chill the target container
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the task requires a container to be cooled with a fridge and the item is not yet in hand. It covers getting the target item from a visible surface or receptacle, then returning to the fridge to chill it.
procedure: Go to the fridge.
Open the fridge if it is closed.
Go to the location that holds the target container.
Pick up the target container.
Return to the fridge.
Cool the held container with the fridge.
constraints: Go to the fridge before cooling, and open it if it is closed.
Pick up the task-relevant container, not another similar item.
Make sure you are holding the item before trying to cool it.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve, clean, and return a target cloth
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a cloth is visible at a known receptacle and it needs to be cleaned with a sinkbasin before being placed back at that receptacle.
procedure: Go to the receptacle that holds the target cloth.
Pick up the cloth from the receptacle.
Go to a sinkbasin.
Clean the cloth with the sinkbasin.
Return to the original receptacle.
Put the cleaned cloth onto the receptacle.
constraints: Only start once the target cloth is visible at the source receptacle.
Hold the cloth before trying to clean it or place it.
Use a sinkbasin for the cleaning step.
After cleaning, navigate back to the intended receptacle before putting the cloth down.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to inspectable storage or support surfaces
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you are searching nearby furniture or containers for a target item, lamp, or place to inspect/put something, and the exact location is not yet confirmed. It applies while moving between candidate locations and checking each one’s visible contents or state.
procedure: Go to a likely candidate location that could hold or support the target.
If the location is a closed container, open it before checking inside.
Inspect the location or its contents to see whether the needed object or fixture is there.
If the needed object is present and reachable, pick it up; if you are carrying the object and this location is the intended destination, place it there.
If the target is not here, continue to the next candidate location and repeat the check.
constraints: Only open a receptacle when it is visibly closed; inspect open containers and surfaces before moving on.
Do not keep rechecking the same location without new information; move to a different candidate when a check finds nothing useful.
Before placing an item, verify that you are holding it and that the destination surface or receptacle is the one you intend to use.

mid_skills.name: Carry an item to the next candidate location
mid_skills.description: Use this when you have just picked up a target item and need to continue the search or move toward another likely source or staging surface. Apply it while relocating between nearby containers, furniture, or surfaces without changing what you are holding.
procedure: Pick up the target item from the current location.
Go directly to the next candidate location that may contain another target item or serve as a temporary staging surface.
If needed, examine the new location or check inventory to confirm what is present and what you are holding before the next manipulation.
constraints: Start this behavior only after the target item has been successfully picked up.
Do not put the held item down during transit unless you intentionally use a visible staging surface for the next step.
If the destination is a closed container, navigate to it first and verify its state before trying to take from or place into it.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and place a visible target item on its destination surface
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a movable target item is visible on an open surface or you are navigating among likely surfaces to collect it and place it onto a known destination surface. It fits repeated fetch-and-deliver placements of the same item type.
procedure: Look around if you have not recently confirmed the room layout or visible surfaces.
Go to a likely surface that may hold the target item.
If the target item is visible there, pick it up from that surface.
Go to the destination surface.
Put the held target item onto the destination surface.
If more matching items are needed, go to another likely surface and repeat the pickup and placement sequence.
constraints: Only try to pick up the item after navigating to a location where it is visibly present.
Only put an item after you are holding it and have reached the destination surface.
Prefer visible open surfaces for this behavior; if the item may be inside a closed receptacle, use a different behavior to access it first.
Do not continue sweeping unrelated locations if you already have a target item in hand; deliver it before resuming search.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve an item from a fridge and carry it to a destination surface
mid_skills.description: Use this when a needed item is stored in a fridge and you must place it on another visible receptacle or surface. Apply it after confirming the item is in the fridge or when opening the fridge is the next step to access it.
procedure: Go to the fridge that should contain the item.
Open the fridge.
Take the target item from the fridge.
Go to the destination surface or receptacle.
Place the item on the destination.
constraints: Navigate to the fridge before trying to open or take from it.
If the fridge is closed, open it before taking the item.
Only try to take the item after it is visible in the open fridge.
Hold the item before attempting to place it at the destination.

mid_skills.name: Collect an item and carry it to the target surface
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when a needed object is visible on a surface or similar location and you need to relocate it to another known destination. It applies once you can navigate to the source and later to the destination.
procedure: Go to the location that holds the needed object.
Pick up the target object from that location.
Check your inventory to confirm you are carrying the object.
Go to the destination location.
Put the object onto the destination surface or receptacle.
constraints: Only pick up the object after you have navigated to its current location and can see it there.
If inventory does not show the object after pickup, do not proceed to the destination; return to the source and reacquire it.
Before putting the object down, verify you are at the intended destination location.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and carry a visible toilet paper item
mid_skills.description: Use this when a toilet paper item needed for placement is visible on a nearby surface or holder, and the current step is to acquire it before moving to the destination receptacle.
procedure: Go to the location holding the toilet paper item.
Pick up the visible toilet paper item from that location.
Check your inventory to confirm you are carrying the toilet paper before proceeding, if needed.
constraints: Navigate to the toilet paper's current location before trying to take it.
Only take the item when it is visibly present at that location.
If you need to confirm what you are holding before continuing, check your inventory after pickup.

mid_skills.name: Carry an item to its target receptacle
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you are already holding an item and need to bring it to a visible destination receptacle or surface to place it there. It also applies when moving back and forth between a source and the same destination for repeated transfers.
procedure: Go to the destination receptacle or surface.
If you are holding the item, place it into or onto the destination.
If more matching items remain elsewhere, return to their source, pick the next item, go back to the same destination, and place it there.
constraints: Only use this after the item has been acquired, or include a pickup step before the transfer loop.
Verify the destination is the intended placement location before putting the item down.
If the destination is inside a container, open it before placing the item.

mid_skills.name: Fetch an item from a source and place it on the target surface
mid_skills.description: Use this when the needed item is visible on one surface or receptacle and the task is to move it onto a different destination surface. Apply it after locating both source and destination.
procedure: Go to the source location that holds the item.
Pick up the needed item from the source.
Go to the destination surface.
Put the item onto the destination.
If more matching items remain to be moved, return to the source and repeat the pickup-and-delivery cycle one item at a time.
constraints: Verify the item is present at the source before picking it up.
Only put an item after navigating to the destination and while holding that item.
If you are already holding an unrelated object, place it somewhere appropriate before picking up the target item.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and place a target bathroom item
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to find a movable bathroom item, optionally prepare it, and place it into a visible destination receptacle. It applies while searching among nearby surfaces or containers and then transferring the item to the goal location.
procedure: Go to likely nearby surfaces or containers that could hold the target item.
Open a closed container before checking or using it as a destination.
When the target item is visible, pick it up from its current surface or receptacle.
If the task requires the item to be clean, go to a sinkbasin and clean the held item.
Go to the destination receptacle or surface.
Place the held item into or onto the destination.
constraints: Only pick up the item after navigating to the place where it is visibly present.
If a destination container is closed, open it before placing the item inside.
Clean the item only when the task specifically requires a clean object.
Avoid extra wandering after locating an eligible item; proceed directly toward preparation or placement.
Verify that you are holding the target item before attempting to clean or place it.

mid_skills.name: Retrieve and relocate a visible bathroom item
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when the needed item is visible on an accessible surface or in an already identified fixture, and you need to move it to another nearby receptacle or surface. Apply it after navigating through likely locations and confirming the item’s current position.
procedure: Go to likely nearby fixtures or surfaces until you find the target item.
Pick up the target item from its current surface or fixture.
Go to the destination surface or receptacle.
Open the destination if it is closed and placement requires access.
Put the item onto or into the destination.
constraints: Only try to pick up the item after you can see it at your current location.
If the destination container is closed, open it before placing the item inside.
If a pickup attempt fails at the current fixture, continue searching other nearby locations instead of repeating the same take action.
Place the item only while holding it and after navigating to the destination.

mid_skills.name: Collect and place target shakers into a cabinet
mid_skills.description: Use this when you need to move multiple small target items from visible surfaces into a cabinet and the items may be spread across more than one location.
procedure: Look around to identify candidate surfaces and the destination cabinet.
Go to a surface that shows a target item and pick up one target item.
Go to another surface with target items and pick up another target item if possible.
Check your inventory to confirm what you are carrying.
Go to the cabinet and put one held target item into it.
If another target item remains at a known surface, go back to that surface, pick it up, return to the cabinet, and put it into the cabinet.
constraints: Verify the target item is visible at the current surface before trying to pick it up.

Check your inventory when collecting multiple items; if you are already holding a different item that blocks pickup, place one carried target item in a convenient visible spot or deliver it before continuing.
Go to the cabinet before placing an item; only put down an item you are currently holding.

mid_skills.name: Navigate to the target item or related fixture
mid_skills.description: Use this behavior when you need to move to a visible task-relevant surface or furniture item so you can manipulate an object there or use a nearby device. Apply it after identifying the likely location of the needed object or fixture.
procedure: Go to the furniture or surface that is likely to hold the needed target object.
If the target object is present there, pick it up.
If you need to verify the object after taking it, examine the held object.
If the needed object or device is not there, go to the next likely nearby surface or furniture item and check again.
constraints: Only pick up an object after you have navigated to the location where it is visibly present.
Use examination as a verification step only when the task requires confirming the held object's identity or details.
If multiple candidate objects are present, do not repeatedly pick up and put down alternatives without a clearer task-specific cue.

mid_skills.name: Carry an item from its current surface to the target receptacle
mid_skills.description: Use this when a movable target item is visible on a surface or fixture and you need to relocate it into or onto a known destination receptacle. Apply it after identifying both the item's current location and the destination.
procedure: Go to the location that holds the target item.
Pick up the target item.
Go to the destination receptacle.
If the destination receptacle is closed, open it.
Place the held item into or onto the destination receptacle.
constraints: Only try to place the item after confirming you are carrying it, using inventory if needed.
Open the destination only when it is visibly closed; do not insert items into a closed receptacle.
If multiple matching items are involved, transport and place them one at a time rather than dropping them at an intermediate location.

high_skills.name: Find, clean, and store the target item
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to locate a specific dirty item, clean it with a sinkbasin, and place that cleaned item into the named destination receptacle.
procedure: Look around and check your inventory to confirm the target item is not already held, then bind the exact target item and destination receptacle from the objective.
Search the likely visible containers or surfaces in the current room until the target item is found; open any closed location before checking its contents.
Go to the location that contains the target item and pick up that item.
Go to a sinkbasin and clean the held target item there.
Go to the specified destination receptacle.
Put the cleaned target item into or onto the destination receptacle to complete the task.
constraints: If the target item is not yet visible, keep searching likely locations and open closed receptacles before inspecting; do not switch to pickup or cleaning until the target is actually seen.
If the clean action is unavailable, first confirm you are holding the target item and are at a sinkbasin, then retry the cleaning step.
If the put action is unavailable at the destination, go to the named destination receptacle while still holding the cleaned target item, then place it there.
Avoid revisiting locations you already checked or closing searched receptacles unless a later required action depends on it.

high_skills.name: Bring the target object to the desk lamp for viewing
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to view or inspect a specific object in the light of a desk lamp, possibly with the object and lamp starting in different locations.
procedure: Identify the target object and the surface that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the desk lamp's surface and confirm the lamp is there.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Go to the target object's current location.
Pick up the target object.
Return to the desk lamp's surface to view it in the light.
constraints: If the lamp's surface does not contain the desk lamp, keep searching for the correct surface before trying to use a lamp.
If the desk lamp is off, switch it on before treating the viewing location as ready.
If the target object is not visible at the expected location, re-check nearby listed surfaces or containers before moving on.
Do not stop after turning on the lamp; completion requires bringing the bound target object back to the lit desk lamp location.
If a needed action is unavailable, restore its prerequisite first: reach the correct location to pick up the object, or reach the lamp's surface to use the lamp.

high_skills.name: Move matching items from one surface to another until all are placed
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to relocate all specified movable items of the same type from a known source surface to a known destination surface, possibly requiring more than one trip.
procedure: Identify the target item type, how many must be moved, the source surface that currently holds them, and the destination surface.
Go to the source surface and pick up as many target items as you can directly from it.
If inventory checking is available after pickup, confirm you are carrying the target item before leaving the source.
Go to the destination surface and put one carried target item onto it.
Track how many target items have been placed at the destination.
If more target items are still required, return to the source surface, pick up the remaining target item from that surface, go back to the destination, and put it onto the destination surface.
Finish only after the required number of target items has been placed on the destination surface.
constraints: If the object at the source does not match the bound target item type, leave it and continue looking only for the goal item.
If inventory does not show a picked item, return to the source surface and reacquire it before going to the destination.
If the put action is unavailable at the destination, first verify you are carrying the target item and are at the correct destination surface, then retry placement.
Do not keep searching other locations or start another trip once you are already carrying a target item; deliver it to the destination first.
When multiple matching items are required, count completed placements and continue the shuttle until that total is reached rather than stopping after the first successful put.

high_skills.name: Switch on the lamp, then inspect and take the pillow
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task requires turning on a desk or bedroom lamp and then identifying or taking the relevant pillow associated with that lit area.
procedure: Identify the lamp that must be turned on and go through likely nearby surfaces until you reach the surface holding that desk lamp.
Confirm the desk lamp is present there and turn it on.
After the lamp is on, go to the associated resting surface or furniture item where the target pillow is likely to be.
Check the local view if needed to confirm the pillow is present, then pick up the pillow.
Examine the held pillow when the task requires confirming it after pickup.
Return to the lamp location if the task's demonstrated completion includes ending there after taking the pillow.
constraints: If the lamp is visible before the target pillow is found, do not start handling other nearby objects first; turn on the required lamp before moving to the pillow phase.
If pickup is unavailable, the pillow is not yet at your current location; continue to the next likely bed, armchair, or similar resting surface instead of repeating pickup.
If examination is unavailable or unnecessary, treat pickup plus the required lamp state as sufficient and continue to the demonstrated completion location.
Do not treat switching on the lamp as completion when the task also requires identifying or taking a pillow; verify the pillow phase is finished before stopping.

high_skills.name: Examine the target object under the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the objective is to inspect a specific item by the light of a desk lamp, which may require finding the item first and bringing it to the lamp if they are not already together.
procedure: Identify the exact target item and the desk lamp location before committing to manipulation.
Search likely visible surfaces and openable containers for the target item until it is found.
Pick up the target item once it is located.
Go to the surface with the desk lamp.
If the target item is not already at the lamp location, place it on that surface so it can be examined there.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Examine the target item under the lamp to complete the task.
constraints: If the target item has not been found yet, keep searching likely locations instead of examining or moving unrelated objects.
If the lamp is visible before you have the target item, do not stop after switching it on; return to the target item, bring it to the lamp, and then examine that same item.

If `examine` on the target item is unavailable or does not satisfy the goal, repair the missing prerequisite by making sure you are at the lamp surface, the lamp is on, and the correct target item is present there.
Stop after the target item has been examined under the lit lamp; moving or inspecting other objects does not advance completion.

high_skills.name: Collect each target bottle and place it in the bathroom bin
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to move the required bottle items from visible bathroom surfaces or fixtures into the bin near the toilet.
procedure: Identify the target bottle type and the destination bin near the toilet before moving items.
Go to each likely surface or fixture where that bottle may be visible.
When the target bottle is visible, pick it up from its current location.
Go to the bin near the toilet.
Put the bottle into the bin.
Repeat the search, pickup, and placement cycle until every required target bottle has been moved into the bin.
constraints: If the item at the current location is a different bottle than the target type, leave it there and continue searching for the correct bottle.
If a pickup action is unavailable or fails, confirm the target bottle is actually visible at the current location before trying again; otherwise move on to another likely location.
Track how many target bottles remain after each successful placement, and do not stop after placing only one when the goal requires more than one.
Only place the bottle after navigating to the bin and while holding that same target bottle.

high_skills.name: Collect each target item and place it into the destination drawer
high_skills.description: Use this when the task is to move multiple specified loose items from their current location to a drawer, ensuring every required item is transferred and the drawer is usable before placement.
procedure: Identify the target item type, how many must be moved, and the destination drawer before acting.
Go to the source location of the target items and pick up the required items there, keeping count of how many are already in hand and how many still need to be moved.
Check inventory after pickup to confirm you are carrying the target item or items before leaving the source.
Go to the destination drawer and open it if it is closed.
Place one carried target item into the drawer.
If more target items still need to be moved, return to the source, pick up the remaining target item, go back to the same drawer, and place it into the drawer.
Finish only after the full required quantity has been placed in the destination drawer.
constraints: If inventory does not show the target item after a pickup attempt, stay focused on reacquiring it at the source before traveling to the drawer.
If the destination drawer cannot accept a put action, open the drawer first instead of retrying placement.
If the task requires more than one item, track how many have already been placed and continue until the full quantity is deposited.
Only place the bound target item into the bound destination drawer; do not substitute a different visible object or a different receptacle.

high_skills.name: Retrieve the target item and inspect it under the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to examine a specific item with a desk lamp: first identify the target item and the desk lamp location, then collect the item if it is elsewhere, bring it to the lamp, and perform the examination there.
procedure: Identify the target item named in the goal and the surface that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the desk lamp surface and examine it to confirm the lamp is there.
Turn on the desk lamp.
If the target item is not already on that surface, search likely locations until you find it and pick it up.
Return to the desk lamp surface while holding the target item.
Place or position the target item at the desk lamp surface if needed for the examination.
Examine the target item with the desk lamp on and stop only after the examination is complete.
constraints: If you have turned on the desk lamp but have not yet identified or collected the target item, treat lamp setup as incomplete and continue searching for the named target item.
If you are interacting with other visible objects on the lamp surface, use them only to clear access when necessary and keep the goal bound to the named target item.
If the target item is not visible at the lamp surface, do not assume it is there; search other likely surfaces or openable containers until you can pick it up.

If an examine action on the target item is unavailable or does not satisfy the task, restore the missing prerequisite by returning to the lit desk lamp surface with the target item before trying again.

high_skills.name: Bring the target object to the desk lamp for viewing
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to view a specified object under a desk lamp, where the lamp and the object may start on different desks.
procedure: Identify both the target object and the desk that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the desk with the desk lamp and turn the lamp on.
Go to the desk or surface holding the target object and pick up that object.
Return to the desk with the lit lamp to complete the viewing task.
constraints: If you have not yet confirmed which desk holds the desk lamp, look around and bind that destination before moving objects.
If the lamp is present but off, turn it on before fetching or presenting the target object there.
If a pickup action is unavailable, first go to the object's current desk or surface and confirm the target object is visible before taking it.
Do not treat switching on the lamp as completion; the task finishes only after the target object has been brought back to the lit desk.

high_skills.name: Find, chill, and discard the target lettuce
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to locate the specified head of lettuce, chill it with the fridge, and then throw it away in the garbage can.
procedure: Identify the target lettuce and the required disposal destination, then use the fridge as the chilling appliance.
Go to the fridge first, open it, and inspect around that area to confirm the fridge and nearby surfaces you may need to search.
Search the relevant nearby surfaces until you find the target lettuce, then pick it up.
Return to the fridge while holding the same lettuce and chill it with the fridge.
Go to the garbage can and place the chilled lettuce into it to complete the task.
constraints: If the target lettuce has not been found yet, keep searching the nearby checked surfaces before attempting to chill or discard anything.
If a cooling action is unavailable, make sure you are holding the target lettuce and are back at the fridge before trying again.
If the garbage-can placement is unavailable, verify you are still holding the chilled target lettuce and are at the garbage can, then place it there.
Do not switch to another object seen near the fridge or garbage can; keep the original target lettuce bound through chilling and disposal.

high_skills.name: Clean, chill, and store the target pot
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to take a specific pot, apply the required cleaning and cooling steps, and place it into the named cabinet.
procedure: Identify the target pot and its destination cabinet before moving it.
Go to the pot's current location and pick up the target pot.
If the task requires cleaning, go to the sinkbasin and clean the held pot there.
Go to the fridge and cool the same pot.
Go to the destination cabinet.
Open the cabinet if it is closed.
Put the cooled pot into the cabinet.
constraints: If the cool action is unavailable, first make sure you are holding the target pot and are at the fridge, then retry.
If the put action is unavailable at the destination, open the cabinet and confirm it is the named destination before placing the pot.
Keep the same target pot bound across cleaning, cooling, and placement; do not switch to a similar item seen at the sink, fridge, or cabinet.
Once you have checked a container or reached a confirmed location, continue forward instead of rechecking the same place unless the target pot is still missing.

high_skills.name: Inspect the target item under the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to examine a specific item with a desk lamp: first identify the target item and the desk lamp location, clear any obstacle to reach the target item, bring the target item to the lamp if needed, switch the lamp on, and examine the target item there.
procedure: Identify the target item and where it currently is, and identify the surface that has the desk lamp.
Go to the target item's location and take the target item once it is visible; if it is inside an openable container, open and examine containers until you find it.
Carry the target item to the desk lamp surface.
Put the target item on the same surface as the desk lamp.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Examine the target item there to complete the task.
constraints: If the target item is not visible where you expect it, search likely containers one at a time by opening and examining each before moving on.
If the lamp is visible before you have the target item, do not stop there; return to fetching the bound target item and bring that item back to the lamp.

If `put` or `examine` is unavailable at the lamp surface, repair the missing prerequisite first: make sure you are holding the target item before placing it, make sure the item is on the lamp surface before examining it, and make sure the lamp is on before the final examination.
Do not examine or shuttle incidental objects at the lamp surface; keep the same target item bound from pickup through the final examination.

high_skills.name: Light the lamp, fetch the target book, and examine it there
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to examine a specific book with a desk lamp by turning on the correct lamp, bringing the target book to that lamp location if needed, and examining the book under the light.
procedure: Identify the target book and the desk lamp location named in the goal.
Go to the surface with the desk lamp, confirm the lamp is there, and turn it on.
Go to the location that holds the target book and pick up that specific book.
Return to the desk lamp surface while still carrying the target book.
Place the target book on the lamp surface.
Examine the target book there to complete the task.
constraints: If the lamp is visible before you have the book, still bind the exact target book and lamp surface first; do not start examining or handling unrelated objects.
If examine or put is unavailable at the lamp surface, restore the missing prerequisite by making sure the target book is in hand or placed on that surface before continuing.
If you pick up or inspect a non-target object while searching, put it back and resume searching for the specified book.
Do not stop after switching on the lamp or after picking up the book; completion requires examining the target book at the lit lamp location.

high_skills.name: Find, clean, and store the target utensil in the cabinet
high_skills.description: Use this when the task is to place a specific dirty item into a cabinet after cleaning it at the sink, starting from not yet knowing exactly where the item is located.
procedure: Look around and check your inventory to bind the target item and confirm you are not already holding it.
Search likely surfaces and containers where the target item may be; open closed locations before checking inside.
When the target item is visible, go to it and pick it up.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the held target item.
Go to the destination cabinet.
Open the cabinet if needed.
Put the cleaned target item into the cabinet.
constraints: If the target item is not yet visible, keep searching likely locations instead of moving on to cleaning or storing.
Only switch from searching to pickup after confirming the visible item matches the target object.
If a container or cabinet is closed, open it before checking contents or placing the item inside.
If clean or put is unavailable, repair the missing prerequisite first: hold the target item, be at the sinkbasin for cleaning, and be at the open destination cabinet for placement.
Do not treat navigation or cleaning alone as completion; finish only after the cleaned target item has been placed into the cabinet.

high_skills.name: Fetch the target item and inspect it under the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to examine a specific item with a desk lamp by first identifying the correct item and lamp location, retrieving the item from its current location, then bringing it back to the lamp-lit surface for the examination.
procedure: Identify the target item to be examined and the surface that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the desk-lamp surface, confirm the lamp is there, and turn the lamp on so the examination area is prepared.
Search the target item's likely location until the item is found; if it is in an openable container, open the container and examine it before taking the item.
Pick up the target item and keep it as the bound goal object while moving back to the desk-lamp surface.
At the desk-lamp surface, examine the target item with the lamp on to complete the task.
constraints: If you have only prepared the lamp area but have not yet acquired the target item, leave the lamp area and continue the item search instead of handling unrelated objects there.
If the target item is not visible where you expect it, inspect the surface or open the relevant container before trying to take it.
If the lamp is on a different surface than the target item, retrieve the target item first or return with it after switching the lamp on; do not treat lighting the lamp as completion.
If the examination action for the target item is unavailable at the lamp surface, recheck that you are holding the correct target item and that the desk lamp is on before retrying.

high_skills.name: Find, chill, and place the target glass
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to place a specified glass or cup after chilling it in the fridge and then putting it on the named destination surface or shelf.
procedure: Identify the target container type and the final destination before moving.
Check likely storage locations until you find the target glass or cup; open a receptacle before inspecting inside it.
When the target container is found, pick it up and keep that same item bound as the goal object.
Go to the fridge and open it if needed.
Cool the held target container with the fridge.
Go to the specified destination surface or shelf.
Put the chilled target container on the destination.
constraints: If a receptacle's contents are unknown, open and inspect it before assuming the target is there or moving on.
If the item at hand is not the required glass or cup, leave it and continue searching for the correct target item.
If the cool action is unavailable, first make sure you are at the fridge, the fridge is open if required, and you are holding the target container.
Do not head to the destination before the target container has been chilled; return to the fridge and complete cooling first.
After checking a storage location that does not contain the target, stop revisiting it and continue to other likely locations.

high_skills.name: Find the apple, warm it, and place it on the target table surface
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to place a warm apple onto the specified table-like destination by first locating the correct apple, heating it, and then delivering it to the destination.
procedure: Identify the target object and destination: the apple must be warmed and then placed on the specified table surface.
Check likely storage first, especially closed food-storage locations; open them before inspecting their contents.
Examine each checked container or surface to confirm whether the apple is there, and continue to other likely locations if it is not.
If you uncover movable items that block visibility or access, relocate only what is necessary to expose the apple, then take the apple once it is confirmed.
Close any storage container you opened before leaving it.
Go to the heating appliance and heat the apple while holding it.
Go to the specified destination table surface and put the warmed apple there.
Treat the placement as complete only after the apple has been moved onto the destination surface.
constraints: If an inspect or take action is unavailable at a closed container, open the container first, then examine it.
If the current location does not contain the apple, do not pick up unrelated items except when needed to reveal the apple; continue checking other likely locations.
If you opened a storage container and are finished there, close it before moving on.
If the apple has been heated but not yet placed, go directly to the destination and complete the final put action instead of stopping at the destination.

high_skills.name: Chill the target item and place it at the destination
high_skills.description: Complete tasks where a specified item must be cooled with the fridge and then delivered to a named surface or receptacle.
procedure: Identify the target item and its required destination.
Go to the target item's current location and pick it up.
Go to the fridge and open it if needed so cooling is possible.
Cool the held target item with the fridge.
Go to the required destination and place the cooled item there.
constraints: If the cooling action is unavailable, first make sure you are at the fridge and the fridge is open, then try again.
Do not leave for the destination until the target item has been cooled; if you are not holding it, return to its location and pick up the correct item.
Keep the goal item bound across the whole task; do not substitute a different item found at the fridge or destination.
If a location has already been checked and the target item is not there, continue searching new likely locations instead of revisiting the same place immediately.

high_skills.name: Move two visible items from the source table to the destination table
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to relocate a specified number of matching movable items from a known source surface to a known destination surface, starting from the initial task state and finishing when all required items have been placed at the destination.
procedure: Identify the target item type, the required count, the source surface, and the destination surface.
Go to the source surface and collect one target item that is visible there.
Go to the destination surface and place the held item onto it.
Track how many target items have been delivered.
If more target items are still required, return to the source surface, collect the next visible target item, go back to the destination surface, and place it there.
Finish only after the full required count has been placed on the destination surface.
constraints: If you are not yet holding a target item, do not go to place at the destination; return to the source surface and pick one up first.
If the put action is unavailable at the destination, verify you are at the correct destination surface while holding the target item, then place it there.
Do not keep searching unrelated locations once you are carrying a target item; deliver it before resuming any search.
Do not stop after the first placement when multiple matching items are required; keep count and repeat until the required number has been delivered.

high_skills.name: Retrieve the target item and switch on the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this when the objective is to collect the specified item from its named starting surface or container and then turn on the desk lamp at the indicated destination area.
procedure: Identify the exact target item and its source location, then go directly to that location.
If the item is inside a closed container, open it so the target is visible.
Take the target item, and if the objective requires multiple copies, collect each required copy before leaving.
Check possession so the target item remains bound as the one you are carrying.
Go to the surface or area that holds the desk lamp.
Confirm the desk lamp is present there, then use it to switch it on.
Treat the lamp turning on as the completion signal for this task.
constraints: If the target item is not visible where expected, inspect the current surface or open the local container before trying to take anything.
If take or use is unavailable, repair the missing prerequisite first: move to the correct location, reveal the container contents, or verify the correct object is in front of you.
If the lamp is visible before the target item has been collected, do not switch it on yet; finish retrieving all required target items first.
If multiple copies are required, track the count and do not leave the source location until all required copies have been collected.

high_skills.name: Find the target item on likely shelves and place it on the destination surface
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to move a specific movable object from its current visible surface in the room to a named destination surface.
procedure: Identify the target object and the destination surface from the goal, then go to the destination once to confirm it before searching.
Look around to bind the room layout and likely source surfaces for the target object.
Search the likely shelves or other open surfaces in an orderly pass until the target object is visibly present.
Pick up the target object from the surface where it is found.
Go directly to the destination surface while keeping the same target object bound.
Put the target object onto the destination surface to complete the move.
constraints: If the target object is not visible at the current surface, continue the ordered search to the next likely surface instead of picking up a different item or acting on the destination early.
If you are already holding the target object, stop searching and deliver it to the destination before doing anything else.
Only use open-surface pickup and placement for this strategy; if the object is inside a closed receptacle, first open and inspect that receptacle before taking the object.
Treat available actions as state checks: if pickup or placement is unavailable, re-verify that you are at the correct surface and that the intended object is visible or in hand before retrying.

high_skills.name: Inspect the target item under a desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the objective is to inspect a specific item by the light of a desk lamp, which may require first finding and carrying the correct item to the lamp's surface.
procedure: Identify the exact target item to be inspected and search likely surfaces or openable containers until you find it.
Pick up the target item and keep it bound as the object to inspect.
If needed, examine the item once to confirm you are holding the correct target.
Go to the surface that has the desk lamp.
If the lamp is on a different surface, place the target item on the lamp's surface so it can be inspected there.
Examine the target item at the lamp location.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Examine the same target item again under the light to complete the inspection.
constraints: If the target item is not visible in a closed drawer, cabinet, or similar container, open it before inspecting or taking anything from it.
If the desk lamp is not on the same surface as the target item, move the target item to the lamp's surface before treating the lamp step as progress.
If using the lamp does not end the task, return to examining the bound target item under the light instead of stopping.
If an expected action is unavailable, repair the missing prerequisite first: navigate to the right surface, open the needed container, or place the item where the lamp can illuminate it.

high_skills.name: Move both target items into the drawer
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to relocate two specified movable items from their current location into a particular drawer.
procedure: Identify the two required target items and the destination drawer before moving anything.
Go to the location holding the target items and pick up each target item that is visible there until you are carrying as many as possible.
Check your inventory to confirm which target items you are holding.
Go to the destination drawer and open it if it is closed.
Place one confirmed target item into the drawer.
If another required target item is still not in the drawer, return to the source location, pick it up, go back to the same drawer, and place it inside.
Finish only after both required target items have been moved into the destination drawer.
constraints: If a required target item is not shown in inventory after pickup, do not head to the drawer with that item assumed secured; return to the source location and reacquire it.
If the destination drawer cannot accept a put action, open the drawer first and then place the item inside.
Track how many required target items have already been deposited; do not stop after placing only one when the goal requires two.
Only place the bound target items into the drawer; do not substitute another visible object from the source or destination area.

high_skills.name: Retrieve, wash, and place the egg in the microwave
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to find the target egg, wash it in the sink, and place the washed egg inside the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target egg and the final destination microwave before acting, then check the most likely storage location for the egg.
Go to the storage receptacle, open it if needed, confirm the target egg is visible, and pick it up.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the egg there.
Go to the microwave, open it if needed, and place the washed egg inside.
constraints: If the egg is not visible in the opened storage receptacle, keep searching other likely locations for that same egg instead of taking a different object.

If a needed action such as pick up or place is unavailable, restore its prerequisite first: open the receptacle, make sure you are at the correct location, and confirm you are holding the egg before moving on.
Do not go to the microwave for placement before washing; after pickup, complete the cleaning step at the sinkbasin before the final transfer.
Only finish after the egg has been moved into the microwave.

high_skills.name: Move the target CD to the desk lamp and switch the lamp on
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to bring a specific CD to the surface with the desk lamp and finish by turning the lamp on there.
procedure: Identify the target CD and the destination surface that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the CD's current location and take that specific CD.
Go to the desk-lamp surface.
Place the CD on that surface.
If needed, reorient to the desk-lamp surface and confirm the desk lamp is there.
Turn on the desk lamp to complete the task.
constraints: Do not act on a different CD just because another one is nearby; keep the goal CD bound from pickup through placement.
Do not try to turn on the lamp before the target CD has been moved onto the lamp's surface; place the CD first, then toggle the lamp.
If the lamp is not visible or usable at the destination, inspect the destination surface again rather than toggling blindly.
Turning on the lamp is the completion step, not placing the CD alone; if the lamp is still off after placement, switch it on.

high_skills.name: Chill the target cup and place it in the coffee maker
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to find the specified cup or mug, chill it with the fridge, and place the chilled item in the coffee maker.
procedure: Identify the target cup or mug and the destination coffee maker before acting.
Check likely visible surfaces or receptacles until you locate the target item.
If the item is in a receptacle, go there and open it if needed, then pick up the target item.
Go to the fridge and cool the held target item with it.
Go to the coffee maker.
Place the chilled target item in the coffee maker.
constraints: If cooling is unavailable, first go to the fridge and open it if needed, then cool the item while holding it.
Do not place any similar cup or mug; verify you are holding the goal item before cooling and before placing.
Do not go to the coffee maker before the item has been chilled; if you arrive early, return to finish cooling first.
If the target is not at the current checked location, continue searching new likely locations rather than repeating checks at the same spot.
End only after the chilled item has been placed in the coffee maker.

high_skills.name: Light the lamp and inspect the target object there
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to inspect a specific object by desk-lamp light on the surface where both the lamp and the object are located.
procedure: Identify the target object named in the goal and search likely open surfaces until you find the surface that has it.
Go to that same surface and confirm the desk lamp is there with the target object.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Examine the surface again under the light to verify the target object is still present.
Take the target object from that lit surface to complete the inspection objective.
constraints: If you find the target object on a surface without the desk lamp, keep the object bound and continue searching for the correct lamp surface before acting on a different item.
If the lamp-use action is unavailable, first go to the surface that contains the desk lamp instead of repeating later-phase actions.
Do not stop after switching on the lamp; confirm the target object on the lit surface and then take that specific object.

Only attempt pickup when the target object is visibly present on the current surface; if it is not visible, re-examine the surface or continue searching new likely surfaces rather than repeating pickup.

high_skills.name: Find the target item and place it in the cabinet
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to locate a named movable item in the room and place that same item into a cabinet.
procedure: Identify the target item and the destination cabinet before moving items.
Search likely locations for the target item, opening cabinets or other openable containers only when needed to inspect them.
When the target item is found, pick up that exact item immediately.
Go to the destination cabinet.
If the destination cabinet is closed, open it.
Place the held target item into the cabinet.
constraints: If the target item is not yet in inventory, continue searching for it instead of going to the cabinet to place something.
If an openable location must be inspected and it is closed, open it before examining or taking anything from it.
If the destination cabinet is closed when you arrive to place the item, open it before attempting to put the item inside.
Once the target item has been picked up, stop the search and carry it directly to the destination cabinet.

high_skills.name: Chill the target cup and place it in the microwave
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to find the specified cup, chill that same cup in the fridge, and place it into the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target object and final destination: the specified cup must be the item you carry through every step, and the microwave is the destination.
Search plausible visible surfaces and openable receptacles until you find the target cup; open a receptacle before taking the cup from it.
Pick up the target cup.
Go to the fridge and open it if needed.
Cool the held cup with the fridge.
Go to the microwave and open it if needed.
Put the chilled cup into the microwave.
constraints: If you encounter a similar container before finding the target cup, do not take it; keep searching until the specified cup is located.
If a needed action is unavailable, fix its prerequisite first: open the fridge or microwave before using it, and make sure you are holding the target cup before cooling or placing it.
After cooling, keep the same chilled cup bound as the goal object and place that item in the microwave; do not substitute an item found in the fridge or at the destination.
Once the chilled cup is in the microwave, stop instead of reopening, removing, or repositioning it unless the placement action has not yet succeeded.

high_skills.name: Clean, chill, and store the target item in a cabinet
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to move a specific item from its current location, wash it in the sinkbasin, chill it in the fridge, and finish by placing it into the target cabinet.
procedure: Identify the target item and the destination cabinet, then orient with a quick look around or inventory check if needed.
Go to the target item's current location and pick it up.
Examine the item if needed to confirm it matches the target.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the held item.
Go to the fridge and cool the same held item there.
Go to the target cabinet and open it if it is closed.
Put the cleaned, chilled item into the cabinet.
constraints: If you are not holding the target item, return to its last known location and pick it up before trying to clean, cool, or store it.
If `clean` or `cool` is unavailable, first go to the required sinkbasin or fridge and repair any missing access prerequisite such as opening the appliance or moving to the correct location.
Do not go to the cabinet just because it is visible; only move to the storage step after the target item has been cleaned and cooled.
When storing, verify you are at the correct cabinet and the target item is still in hand; if the cabinet is closed, open it before putting the item inside.
Do not stop after cleaning or cooling; completion is placing the target item into the cabinet.

high_skills.name: Find, heat, and refrigerate the target plate
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to place a specific plate into the fridge after first locating it and heating it in the microwave.
procedure: Search the likely visible surfaces in the current area until you find the target plate, then pick it up and confirm it is the correct plate.
Go to the microwave and heat the plate while holding it.
Go to the fridge.
Open the fridge if it is closed.
Place the heated plate inside the fridge.
constraints: If the target plate is not at the current surface, continue checking other likely surfaces instead of picking up a different object.
Do not go to the fridge for the final placement until the target plate has been heated.
If the fridge does not allow placement yet, open it before trying to put the plate inside.
