After placing the plate in the fridge, stop; do not take it back out or perform extra inspection steps.

high_skills.name: Find the box and place it on the chair
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the objective is to locate a specific movable item in the room and place it onto the named chair or similar target surface.
procedure: Identify the destination surface first so the target placement location stays fixed.
Scan the room and check likely visible surfaces and containers for the target item.
When checking a closed container, go to it, open it, and examine its contents before deciding whether the target is there.
As soon as the correct target item is visible and reachable, pick it up from its current location.
If you opened a container and no longer need it, close it before leaving that area.
Carry the same target item to the destination chair.
Place the item on the destination surface and stop after the placement succeeds.
constraints: If the target item is not visible at the current location, do not try to pick it up there; continue to the next unchecked candidate location.
If a needed container action is unavailable, first go to that container and repair its state by opening it before examining or taking anything.
Do not switch to an incidental object found near the destination or inside a checked container; keep the original target item bound until placement is complete.
Do not leave an opened container behind once you are done checking it; close it before moving on unless you need immediate further access there.

high_skills.name: Find, microwave, and discard the target item
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to place a specified item into a trash receptacle after heating it in the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target item and the required destination trash receptacle before acting.
Check likely storage locations and visible surfaces for the target item; if a container is closed, open it first.
When you find the target item, pick it up from that confirmed location.
Go straight to the microwave while keeping the target item in hand.
Heat the held target item with the microwave.
Go directly to the destination trash receptacle.
Place the heated target item into the trash receptacle.
constraints: If a take or inspect action is unavailable at a container, open that container before searching or picking.
Do not leave for the microwave until you have confirmed and picked up the target item; if unsure, verify your inventory.
If the current location does not contain the target item, continue searching other likely locations instead of taking unrelated objects.
After heating, go directly to the destination receptacle and only place the item once you are at that receptacle.

high_skills.name: Find the target item and place it in the cabinet
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to locate a specified movable item in the room and put that item into a cabinet.
procedure: Identify the target item and the cabinet that will receive it before moving anything.
Check likely locations until the target item is found; if a container is closed, open it before inspecting inside.
When the target item becomes visible, pick it up immediately and stop searching.
Go straight to the target cabinet while keeping the same item bound as the goal object.
If the cabinet is closed, open it.
Place the carried target item into the cabinet.
constraints: Do not try to place any item until you have confirmed you are carrying the named target item; if unsure, verify via inventory and recover the correct item first.
Do not inspect or take from a closed container; open it first, then verify whether the target item is visible inside.
Do not stop after opening candidate cabinets or after finding the item in another location; completion requires putting the target item into the cabinet.
If the put action is unavailable at the cabinet, check the missing prerequisite: be at the correct cabinet, have it open if needed, and still be carrying the target item.

high_skills.name: Find, heat, and place the target egg
high_skills.description: Locate the goal egg from likely kitchen storage or surfaces, heat that same egg in the microwave, and place it at the specified destination receptacle or surface.
procedure: Identify the target object as the egg and bind its required final destination before moving it.
Check the fridge first; open it and inspect for the egg.
If the egg is not there, search other likely kitchen surfaces or receptacles one by one until the egg is found.
Pick up the egg only after it is visible at the current location.
Go to the microwave and heat the egg.
Carry the heated egg directly to the bound destination.
Place the heated egg on or in the destination to complete the task.
constraints: If you cannot heat, verify you are holding the egg; if not, return to the search and pickup phase for the egg rather than heating another object.
If the egg is not visible in a closed receptacle, open it first; if it is still absent, continue the location search instead of taking a different item.
After heating, do not stop at the destination without placing the egg; perform the final put action to complete the task.
If you have picked up a non-egg item, set it aside only as needed to recover possession of the egg, then continue with the egg as the bound target.
Once the egg is heated, go straight to the destination instead of reopening unrelated containers or revisiting checked locations.

high_skills.name: Collect bottles and place them in the bathroom bin
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to gather the required bottle items from their current locations and place each one into the bin near the toilet until all required bottles have been transferred.
procedure: Identify the target item type and the destination bin near the toilet before moving items.
Go to a location that contains one of the target bottles and pick it up.
If you must free your hands to take another target bottle, place the currently held non-target or already-relocated item on a nearby surface, then continue with the target bottle.
Carry the target bottle to the bathroom bin near the toilet and place it inside.
If additional target bottles remain in other known locations, go to each source in turn, pick the next target bottle, return to the same bin, and place it there.
Finish only after every required bottle has been placed in the bin.
constraints: If the visible bottle is not the goal bottle type, leave it and continue searching for the required bottle type before transporting anything.
If a put action into the bin is unavailable, first go back to the bin location and confirm you are holding the target bottle, then place it.
If you are holding a different item when you need to pick up a target bottle, set the other item down on a nearby surface and resume the transfer loop with the target bottle.
Do not stop after placing one bottle when more matching bottles remain; keep count and continue until all required bottles are in the bin.

high_skills.name: Find, transform, and place the target vessel
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task requires a specific cup, mug, bowl, or similar container to be located from its current holder or surface, given the required temperature change if any, and then placed into the named destination receptacle or onto the named destination surface.
procedure: Identify the exact target vessel and the final destination before acting.
Search the likely holders and surfaces that can contain the target vessel; when you reach a closed container or appliance, open it before checking or taking the item.
Take the target vessel once found and keep that same item bound as the task object.
If the goal requires heating, go to the microwave, place the vessel inside if needed, heat it, then open the microwave and retrieve the heated vessel before leaving.
If the goal requires chilling, go to the fridge and chill the vessel while holding it or using the fridge as allowed, then leave with the same chilled vessel.
Go to the named destination receptacle or surface, open it first if it is closed, and place the transformed target vessel there.
Treat the placement action as the completion step; do not continue moving the item afterward.
constraints: If a heat, cool, or place action is unavailable, fix its prerequisite instead of repeating it: acquire the target item, open the needed container/appliance, or move to the correct destination first.
Do not switch to a different nearby vessel after pickup or after transformation; the same bound target item must be the one placed at the destination.
Do not stop after heating, cooling, or an initial placement without the task-specified destination placement confirmed; if you have already placed it correctly, leave it there.
Do not perform extra retrievals or re-place the item after successful destination placement; unnecessary rehandling led away from completion in failed attempts.

high_skills.name: Chill the target item and place it on the table
high_skills.description: Complete tasks where a specified item must be found, chilled with the fridge, and then placed on the target table surface.
procedure: Identify the target item and the destination table named in the goal.
Check the fridge first to confirm whether the item is already inside; open and examine it, then close it when finished.
If the item is not in the fridge, search the named surfaces in the current area until you find the bound target item and pick it up.
Return to the fridge and use it to chill the item.
Go to the destination table and place the chilled item there.
constraints: If the fridge interaction you need is unavailable, go to the fridge first; if it is closed, open it before examining or chilling the item.
If examining the fridge shows the target item is not there, do not treat other visible items as the goal; keep searching for the bound target item on nearby surfaces.
Do not leave the fridge open after checking it when you still need to search elsewhere; close it before moving on.
Only place the item after the cooling action has been completed; if placement is available before cooling, return to the fridge and chill the same held item first.

high_skills.name: Chill the target glass and place it on the table
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to identify the required glass, chill that same glass with the fridge, and place it onto the specified table.
procedure: Identify the target glass and the destination table before acting on a candidate item.
Go to the target glass's current location and pick up that glass.
Go to the fridge and open it if needed.
Cool the held glass with the fridge.
Go to the destination table.
Put the chilled glass on the table.
Confirm the target glass is no longer in hand; if multiple matching glasses are required, repeat the same pickup-cool-place cycle for the next one.
constraints: If the fridge's cool action is unavailable, first go to the fridge and open it, then retry cooling the held glass.
If you reach the table holding an item that is not the target glass, keep the goal bound to the required glass and fetch the correct item before placing anything.
Only put the item down after it has been cooled; if the glass is still unchilled, return to the fridge with it before delivery.
If the target glass is not at the expected location, search other likely visible surfaces or open receptacles until you find that same glass, then resume the chill-and-place sequence.

high_skills.name: Find, transform, and place the target cup
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to handle a specific cup or mug by first locating the target item, applying the required temperature change with the correct appliance, and then placing it in the named destination receptacle or surface.
procedure: Identify the target cup or mug and the required destination before acting, then search likely visible surfaces or containers until you find that exact item.
Go to the target item's location and open the container if needed, then pick up the target cup or mug.
Go to the required appliance for the task and apply the needed temperature change to the held item.
After the state change is complete, go directly to the named destination.
Open the destination if needed and place the transformed target item there.
Treat the placement itself as completion; if available, confirm by checking that the item is now in or on the destination.
constraints: If a heating or cooling action is unavailable, first repair its prerequisite: make sure you are holding the target item and are at the correct appliance before trying again.
If the destination is closed, open it before placing the item; if placement is unavailable, recheck that you are at the intended destination rather than a nearby appliance or surface.
Do not start at the appliance just because it is visible; bind and retrieve the task-specified cup or mug first.
Do not switch to a different similar cup or mug during search or placement; keep the same target item bound from pickup through final placement.
Once the item has been transformed, go straight to the destination instead of reopening, reexamining, or retrieving it again unless placement fails and a missing prerequisite must be fixed.

high_skills.name: Find the tissue box and place it on the coffee table
high_skills.description: Move the target tissue box from its unknown starting surface to the specified coffee table.
procedure: Identify the target object and destination at the start: the tissue box must end up on the coffee table.
Scan visible surfaces in the room, then check plausible tables one by one until the tissue box is found.
When the tissue box is visible on a surface, go to that surface and pick it up.
Go directly to the coffee table while keeping the same tissue box as the bound target.
Place the tissue box on the coffee table and stop after the put action succeeds.
constraints: If the tissue box is not visible at the current location, continue to the next plausible surface instead of repeating checks at the same empty spot.
If you are already holding the tissue box, do not keep searching other locations; go straight to the coffee table and place it.
If the put action is unavailable or fails, first confirm you are at the coffee table and still holding the tissue box, then retry the placement there.

high_skills.name: Find the egg, microwave it, then throw it away
high_skills.description: Complete tasks where the goal is to locate the egg, heat it in the microwave, and place the heated egg in the garbage can.
procedure: Identify the target object as the egg and the final destination as the garbage can before acting.
Check the fridge first by going to it, opening it, and examining its contents for the egg.
If the egg is not in the fridge, search the visible countertops until you find the egg.
Pick up the egg once it is visible at the current location.
Go to the microwave and heat the egg.
Go directly to the garbage can while keeping hold of the heated egg.
Place the heated egg into the garbage can.
constraints: If you see a different food item while searching, do not substitute it; continue searching until you find the egg.
If pickup is unavailable, first open or examine the current receptacle or continue to another likely egg location.
If heating is unavailable, make sure you are holding the egg and return to the microwave before trying again.
After heating, do not reopen containers or handle other objects; carry the same heated egg straight to the garbage can and place it there.

high_skills.name: Fetch the target book before using the lamp
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to inspect a specific book with a lamp-lit surface: first identify and collect the target book from its current location, then bring it to the relevant desk or table, light that surface, place the book there, and examine it under the lamp to complete the task.
procedure: Identify the target book and the lamp-equipped surface named in the task.
Go to the book's current location and pick up that specific book.
Go to the lamp-equipped surface.
If the lamp is off, turn it on.
Place the book on the surface under the lamp.
Look at or examine the surface so the placed book is visible there.
Examine the target book to complete the task.
constraints: If you are interacting with other items on the lit surface before you have the target book, stop and go retrieve the target book first.
If the target book is not in hand when you reach the lamp surface, do not start examining or rearranging surface items; return to the search phase and get the book.
If the book is not visible on the surface after placing it, look or examine the surface before trying to examine the book.
If an action such as placing or examining the book is unavailable, repair the missing prerequisite: pick the book up, move to the correct surface, or turn the lamp on before retrying.

high_skills.name: Clear held item, collect the target bottles, and place them in the toilet-side bin
high_skills.description: For tasks where the goal is to place bottle items into the bin near the toilet, first free your hands if you are carrying a different bottle, then gather the correct bottle items from their visible source locations and place each one into the target bin until no required bottles remain.
procedure: Identify the target item type and the destination bin near the toilet before moving items.
If you are already holding a different bottle when you find the goal bottles, go to a safe visible surface and put the held bottle down so your hands are free.
Go to the visible source surface or receptacle that contains a goal bottle and pick up one target bottle.
Go to the bin near the toilet and place the bottle into it.
Return to the source location for the next matching bottle, pick it up, and bring it back to the same bin.
Repeat the transfer loop until all required goal bottles from the identified source locations have been placed in the bin.
constraints: If you are holding a non-goal item when you need to pick up a goal bottle, first place the non-goal item on a visible surface, then resume collecting the goal bottle.
Only pick up a bottle after confirming it matches the goal item type; do not treat another bottle already at the destination or in your hand as the target.
Place a bottle only after navigating to the bin near the toilet; if put is unavailable, restore the missing prerequisite by going back to the destination or opening it if access is blocked.
When repeating the task, return to locations that previously showed matching bottles instead of rechecking unrelated places.

high_skills.name: Collect matching bottles and place them in the bathroom bin
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to gather the required bottle items from their current bathroom locations and place each one into the designated garbage can near the toilet until all required matching bottles are deposited there.
procedure: Identify the target item type and the destination garbage can near the toilet before moving items.
Go to a likely location for a target bottle and take one when it is visible and accessible.
If you are already holding a non-target item or need to free your hands, place that item on a nearby surface first, then continue with the target bottle.
Carry the target bottle to the garbage can and put it into the bin.
Return to other likely bottle locations, pick up the next matching bottle, and repeat the transfer until all required matching bottles have been placed in the same garbage can.
Finish only after the last matching bottle has been placed in the garbage can.
constraints: If the visible bottle is not the goal item type, do not carry it to the bin; keep searching until a matching target bottle is found.
If you cannot pick up an item at the current location, treat that as evidence the target is not available there now and continue to other likely locations instead of repeating the same pickup.
If your hands are occupied when you need the target bottle, first put the held item on a nearby surface, then pick up the target bottle and resume delivery to the bin.
Put items into the destination only while holding them and after navigating to the identified garbage can.

high_skills.name: Wash an egg and place it in the microwave
high_skills.description: Find the specified egg, wash it in the sinkbasin, and place the washed egg inside the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target egg and its required destination, then search the most likely storage receptacle for that egg.
Go to the receptacle containing the egg and open it if needed.
Confirm the egg is visible, then pick up the target egg.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the egg.
Go to the microwave and open it if needed.
Put the washed egg into the microwave.
constraints: If the target egg is not visible in the opened receptacle, keep searching other plausible locations instead of taking a different item.
If a take or put action is unavailable, first repair the missing prerequisite: open the receptacle or ensure you are holding the egg before continuing.
Do not go to the microwave before washing the egg; clean it at the sinkbasin immediately after pickup.
Only finish after the egg has been placed inside the microwave.

high_skills.name: Wash the target cup and place it on the specified surface
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to find the named cup, wash it at the sinkbasin, and place that cleaned cup onto the specified destination surface.
procedure: Check your inventory and surroundings to confirm the target object and destination, then go to the cup's current location.
If the cup is inside a receptacle, open that receptacle, then pick up the target cup.
Go to the sinkbasin and clean the held cup there.
Go to the specified destination surface.
Place the cleaned cup on that destination.
constraints: Before picking anything up, confirm it is the target cup rather than a similar object; if you grabbed the wrong item, put it back and retrieve the correct cup.
Do not try to clean until the target cup is in hand and you are at the sinkbasin; if clean is unavailable, first restore the missing holding or location prerequisite.
Do not stop after washing; after cleaning, navigate to the named destination and place the same cleaned cup there to finish.
If the destination or source receptacle is closed, open it before taking from it or placing into it.

high_skills.name: Light the desk lamp and bring the target item there for examination
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to examine a specific item with a desk lamp by finding the item, preparing the lamp location, moving the item to that lit surface, and completing the examination there.
procedure: Identify the target item to be examined and the desk or other surface that holds the desk lamp.
Go to the lamp surface, examine it to confirm the desk lamp is there, and switch the desk lamp on.
If the target item is already on that surface, keep it bound as the object to examine; otherwise search likely locations until you find the target item and pick it up.
Return to the lit lamp surface while holding the target item.
Put the target item onto the lamp surface if needed, then examine the target item under the lamp to complete the task.
constraints: If the lamp surface contains many objects and the target item is not among them, do not examine incidental objects there; leave to locate the bound target item and bring it back.
If the target item is inside a closed container, open the container and verify the item is visible before taking it.
If `examine` for the target item is unavailable at the lamp surface, repair the missing prerequisite by ensuring the lamp is on and the target item is present there before trying again.
Do not stop after switching on the lamp or after picking up the item; completion requires examining the target item with the lamp.

high_skills.name: Chill the target item and place it on the destination surface
high_skills.description: Complete tasks where a specified item must be located from its starting surface or container, chilled using the fridge, and then placed on the named destination surface or receptacle.
procedure: Identify the target item and its required final destination before moving it.
Go to the target item's current location and pick it up.
Go to the fridge and open it if cooling requires access.
Cool the held target item with the fridge.
Go to the specified destination surface or receptacle.
Put the chilled target item at the destination.
constraints: If the target item's location is uncertain or it may be stored inside a receptacle, open and examine that location before trying to take it.
If the cool action is unavailable, first make sure you are at the fridge and holding the correct target item; open the fridge if needed, then retry.
Keep the goal object bound across all steps; do not substitute a similar item found at the fridge or destination.
After placing the item, stop only when the put action has succeeded at the specified destination.

high_skills.name: Move matching items from one surface to another until all are placed
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to relocate a specified set or quantity of matching movable items from a known source surface to a known destination surface, potentially requiring multiple trips to finish placing them all.
procedure: Identify the target item type, how many are required, the source surface, and the destination surface before moving items.
Go to the source surface and pick up the target item or items that are visibly there.
If you pick something up, confirm you are carrying the intended target item before leaving for delivery.
Go to the destination surface and place one carried target item there.
Track how many target items have been successfully placed at the destination.
If more target items are still required, return to the source surface, pick up the next remaining target item, go back to the destination, and place it.
Finish only after the required quantity of the bound target items has been placed on the destination surface.
constraints: If the visible item at the source does not match the bound target item type, leave it and keep searching for the correct item.
If inventory does not show the target item after a pickup attempt, stay with or return to the source surface and reacquire it before going to the destination.
If you are already carrying a target item, do not continue searching other locations; go directly to the destination and place it first.
If the put action is unavailable at the destination, verify you are at the correct destination surface while still holding the target item, then place it there.
When multiple items are required, count completed placements and continue the source-to-destination loop until the full quantity is delivered.

high_skills.name: Place toilet paper in the waste basket
high_skills.description: Use this when the objective is to find the specified toilet paper item, pick it up from its current holder or surface, carry it to the waste basket, and place it there.
procedure: Identify the target toilet paper and its destination waste basket before moving items.
Go to the location holding the target toilet paper.
Pick up the target toilet paper.
Go to the waste basket.
Place the toilet paper into the waste basket.
constraints: If the toilet paper is not visible when you arrive, search its current holder or surface until the target item is found before attempting pickup.
If pickup is unavailable, first move to the toilet paper's exact location and confirm the target item is present, then take it.
If the waste basket is a closed container, open it before placing the toilet paper inside.
If the put action is unavailable at the waste basket, verify you are still carrying the target toilet paper, then place that same item into the waste basket.

high_skills.name: Light the desk lamp, then inspect and take the target item
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the objective is to inspect a specific item by desk-lamp light and obtain that same item from the surface where the lamp is used.
procedure: Identify the target item to be inspected and the desk lamp that will provide the required light.
Go to the original location of the target item and confirm what object you are looking for.
Go to the surface that holds the desk lamp.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Examine the lamp's surface to verify the target item is there under the light.
Take the target item from that surface to complete the inspection objective.
constraints: If the desk lamp is visible but you have not yet confirmed the target item you need, bind the target item first by checking its original location or description before acting at the lamp.
If turning on the lamp does not make the target item available, examine the lamp's surface and nearby described contents before taking anything; do not substitute a different object.
Do not treat switching on the lamp as completion; finish by taking the target item from the lit surface.

high_skills.name: Inspect an item under a desk lamp and take it from the lit surface
high_skills.description: Use this when you need to bring a specific portable item to a desk or side surface with a desk lamp, inspect it under the lamp, and finish by taking the same item from that lit surface.
procedure: Identify the target item and the destination surface that has the desk lamp.

After turning on the lamp, do not treat that as completion; the target bowl must still be brought to the lit lamp surface.
Before final placement, verify you are at the desk lamp's surface; if not, navigate back there with the bowl.

high_skills.name: Find the target item and place it in the cabinet
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to put a specific movable item into a cabinet, and the item's exact starting container or surface is not yet known.
procedure: Identify the target item and the destination cabinet before acting.
Check likely cabinets first: go to each cabinet one at a time, and open it if it is closed to see whether the target item is inside.
If the target item is not in those cabinets, continue searching other visible locations until you find it.
When the target item is visible, pick it up immediately.
Go to the destination cabinet.
If the destination cabinet is closed, open it.
Place the target item into the cabinet.
constraints: Do not try to place any item until inventory confirms you are holding the target item; if not, resume the search and pickup phase.
If a cabinet must be inspected or used for placement and it is closed, open it before examining or putting anything inside.
Once the target item is found, stop opening unrelated containers and carry it directly to the destination cabinet.
If the target item is not visible in the current cabinet after opening it, move on to the next likely location instead of repeating the same check.

high_skills.name: Find the book and examine it under the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the task is to inspect a specific book using a desk lamp, starting from the room state before either the book or the lamp has been handled.
procedure: Identify the desk or surface that has the desk lamp and go there.
Examine that surface to confirm the desk lamp is present, then switch the lamp on.
If the target book is not on that lit surface, search other likely locations until you find the book, then pick it up.
Return with the book to the desk lamp surface.
Examine the book under the lamp to complete the task.
constraints: If the lamp is visible but the target book has not been found yet, do not stop at turning on the lamp; continue searching for the book and bring it back to the lamp.
If you start inspecting or picking up other objects on the lamp surface, refocus on locating the target book instead of cycling through unrelated items.
If examine or inspection of the book is not available at the lamp location, first ensure you are holding the target book and are back at the lit desk lamp surface.

high_skills.name: Find the target item, inspect it, then switch on the desk lamp
high_skills.description: Use this when the task is to locate a specific small object and complete the objective by inspecting it and turning on a desk lamp associated with the required surface or nearby furniture.
procedure: Identify the target object and the desk-lamp area named in the task, then go first to the most likely surface holding the target object.
At each checked surface, verify whether the target object is visible; when you find it, pick it up.
Examine the held target object if the task requires confirming or inspecting it.
If the task requires the object at the lamp surface, carry it to that surface, place it there, inspect the surface, and pick the same target object back up once it is visible there.
Navigate through likely nearby furniture or surfaces until you reach the one that has the desk lamp.
Confirm the desk lamp is present at that location, then use the desk lamp to turn it on and finish when the light is on.
constraints: If the target object is not visible on the current surface, do not try to pick it up there; move to the next likely surface and check again.

If a required action such as pickup, placement, or lamp use is unavailable, repair the missing prerequisite first: reach the correct location, make the object visible on the surface, or confirm the lamp is the one at the task-relevant location.
If you place the target object on a surface for the task, keep that same object bound and re-verify it before picking it back up or proceeding.
Do not treat examining the object or reaching the lamp location as completion; the task ends only after the desk lamp has been switched on.

high_skills.name: Retrieve, microwave, and discard the target apple
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to find the specified apple, heat it in the microwave, and place the heated apple into the trash can.
procedure: Identify the target apple and its destination, then go to the most likely storage location that could contain it.
If that location is closed, open it and check for the target apple there.
Take the target apple once you have confirmed its location.
Go to the microwave and heat the apple.
Go directly to the trash can.
Put the heated apple into the trash can.
constraints: If a container is closed, open it before trying to take or inspect the target apple.
If the target apple is not at the current location, keep searching other likely locations instead of picking up a different object.
If heating is unavailable, first make sure you are holding the target apple and are at the microwave.
After heating, keep the same target apple bound and carry it straight to the trash can before placing it.

high_skills.name: Light the desk lamp and bring the target object back for viewing
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to inspect a specific object under a desk lamp, and the object and the lamp may start on different desks or surfaces.
procedure: Identify both the target object and the desk or surface that has the desk lamp.
Go to the desk lamp's surface and confirm the desk lamp is there.
Turn on the desk lamp.
Go to the target object's current location.
Pick up the target object.
Return to the lit desk-lamp surface to view the object there.
constraints: If you have not yet identified which surface holds the desk lamp, search for that surface before trying to inspect the object.
If the desk lamp is visible but off, turn it on before fetching or presenting the target object for viewing.
If the target object is on a different surface than the lamp, keep the target object bound and return it to the lit lamp surface after picking it up.
If pickup or viewing-related progress is unavailable, re-check that you are at the correct object location or back at the lit desk-lamp surface instead of repeating later-phase actions.

high_skills.name: Heat the target item and place it in the fridge
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to find the specified item, heat it with the microwave, and store that heated item inside the fridge.
procedure: Identify the target item and search likely visible surfaces or containers until you locate that exact item.
If the item is inside a closed container, open the container and confirm the item is there.
Pick up the target item and keep it bound as the object to transform and deliver.
Go to the microwave and heat the held target item.
Go to the fridge.
Open the fridge if it is closed.
Place the heated target item inside the fridge.
constraints: If heating is unavailable, first make sure you are holding the target item and are at the microwave.
Do not use the fridge to cool the item after heating; once it is heated, open the fridge and place it inside.
If placing into the fridge is unavailable, open the fridge first rather than repeating the place action.
After placing the item in the fridge, stop; do not take it back out or continue manipulating it.

high_skills.name: Collect bathroom bottles and place them in the toilet-side bin
high_skills.description: Move the required bottle items from their starting bathroom surfaces or fixtures into the bin near the toilet, repeating the transfer until all needed matching bottles have been placed there.
procedure: Identify the target bottle type and the destination bin near the toilet before moving items.
Search likely bathroom fixtures and surfaces for a target bottle, and when one is visible, pick it up.
If you are already holding a different item, free your hands by setting that non-target item down at the current work surface before continuing with the target bottle.
Go to the bin near the toilet and place the held target bottle into it.
If more matching bottles remain in previously checked source locations, return to those source surfaces, pick up the next target bottle, go back to the same bin, and place it there.
Finish only after the required bottle items have been transferred into the bin.
constraints: If the visible bottle is not the goal bottle type, do not carry it to the bin; keep searching for the correct item.
If you cannot pick up a target bottle because your hands are occupied, put down the other held item first, then retry the target pickup.
Place an item into the destination only while holding the target bottle and after navigating to the bin near the toilet.
If the destination is a closed receptacle, open it before placing the bottle inside.
If a pickup fails at one surface, continue searching other likely bathroom locations instead of repeating the same take action.

high_skills.name: Find, warm, and store the target item
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to place a specified item in a cupboard or cabinet after warming it with the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target item and its required destination, then search likely surfaces and containers until you locate that exact item.
Go to the target item's location and take it.
Go to the microwave and warm the item there.
Go to the specified cupboard or cabinet.
Open the destination if it is closed.
Place the warmed item into the destination.
constraints: If you have not yet found and picked up the exact target item, do not go to the microwave or destination to act on an incidental object; continue the search until the target is bound.
If the destination placement action is unavailable, check whether the cupboard or cabinet is closed and open it before placing the item.
If warming is unavailable at the microwave, first ensure you are holding the target item and are at the microwave, then retry the warming step.
When searching, do not revisit locations you already checked unless new evidence points there; move on to the next likely location instead of wandering between unrelated appliances and fixtures.

high_skills.name: Microwave a found apple and discard it
high_skills.description: Use this when the objective is to locate the specified apple, heat it in the microwave, and place the heated apple into the trash can.
procedure: Identify the target apple and the final trash can before acting.
Go to a likely storage location that can contain the apple.
Open the location if it is closed, then take the target apple once it is found there.
Go directly to the microwave and heat the apple.
Go to the trash can.
Put the heated apple into the trash can.
constraints: If the current container is closed, open it before trying to take the apple.
If the apple is not in the current location, keep searching other likely locations and do not pick up unrelated objects.
If heating is unavailable, first make sure you are holding the target apple and are at the microwave.
After heating, go straight to the trash can and only place the apple when you have reached it.

high_skills.name: Collect target items and place them into the drawer
high_skills.description: Use this when the task is to move one or more specified items from their current locations into a particular drawer, starting from an unknown item layout and requiring confirmation by placing the needed quantity into the destination.
procedure: Identify the target item type, required quantity, and the destination drawer before moving anything.
Scan the room and check plausible surfaces or receptacles until you locate a target item.
When a target item is visible and reachable, pick it up and keep track of how many required items you have already secured or delivered.
Go to the destination drawer, open it if it is closed, and place one target item into it.
If more target items are still needed, return to search for another target item and repeat the pickup-and-delivery cycle until the required quantity has been placed in the drawer.
constraints: If the destination drawer will not accept a put action, verify you are at the correct drawer and open it before trying again.
If a location does not contain the target item, continue to a new candidate location instead of rechecking the same empty spot.
Only place items that match the bound target object; if you are holding an extra unrelated object, set it aside and continue with the target items.
Do not stop after collecting items or after opening the drawer; finish only after the required number of target items has been placed into the drawer.

high_skills.name: Move the target item under the desk lamp and inspect it
high_skills.description: Use this when the goal is to inspect a specific item with a desk lamp by finding the item, moving it onto the lamp's surface if needed, and finishing with the lamp turned on for examination.
procedure: Identify the target item and the desk lamp's surface.
Go to the item's current location and take the target item.
Go to the desk lamp's surface.
Place the target item on that surface.
Confirm the desk lamp is there and turn it on.
Examine the target item with the lamp on to complete the task.
constraints: If the desk lamp is on a different surface than the target item, move the target item to the lamp's surface before treating the lighting step as sufficient.

If a needed action is unavailable, check its prerequisite state first: be at the correct location to pick or put, be holding the target item before putting it, and be at the lamp's surface before toggling the lamp.
Do not stop after turning on the lamp or after placing the item; finish by examining the target item under the lit lamp.
If you are holding an unrelated object when you need to take the target item, put the unrelated object down in an appropriate place before continuing.

high_skills.name: Find, heat, and place the target dish on the dining table
high_skills.description: Use this strategy when the goal is to place a specified dish item on the dining table after warming it in the microwave.
procedure: Identify the target item named in the goal and the final destination dining table before moving items.
Search likely visible surfaces first until the target item is found; examine a container before taking from it if the item may be inside.
Pick up the confirmed target item and keep it bound as the object to transform and deliver.
Go to the microwave and heat the held target item.
Go to the dining table.
Put the heated target item on the dining table.
Confirm the item is now resting on the dining table as the completion condition.
constraints: If you have not yet confirmed the target item's location, keep checking new likely locations instead of wandering between appliances and unrelated receptacles.
If the target is inside a closed container, open it and verify the item is there before trying to take anything.
If the heat action is unavailable, first ensure you are holding the target item and are at the microwave, then retry.
If you are holding or inspecting a different object, put it back or ignore it and resume searching for the named target item.
Do not stop after heating; the task is only complete once the heated target item has been placed on the dining table.
